page: UpCloud PREMIUM-38xCPU-192GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 38 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 5.05 GB of RAM per core. The underlying processor is an AMD EPYC 9575F x86_64 CPU operating at 2.0 GHz, featuring 38 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core and a shared CPU allocation. The processor cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache. The server is bundled with 500 GB of local SSD storage and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations place this server in the top tier for single-core CPU performance via stress-ng, single-threaded gzip compression, single-threaded bzip3 decompression, and database operations. It achieves strong performance in multi-core CPU stress tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and overall PassMark CPU scores. Memory performance is robust, with small-block read-only bandwidth ranking in the top tier, while large-block read and write bandwidths register in the top 25%. For web serving and key-value storage, the system delivers strong throughput in static web serving and Redis SET operations.

The shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated physical core performance, though the high core count and AMD EPYC architecture mitigate this for parallel tasks. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density of 38 cores and 192 GB RAM paired with local SSD storage provides a balanced profile for multi-tenant environments and medium-scale enterprise deployments. This server is highly suited for database operations, high-throughput web serving, parallel data compression, and moderate-scale language model inference.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-38xCPU-192GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 38 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes a shared CPU allocation, 500 GB of local SSD storage, and one public IPv4 address. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES cryptography. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in single-core processing, database operations, and small-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core and large-block memory throughput. While the shared CPU model presents a minor tradeoff for dedicated compute-bound tasks, the high core count and memory capacity offer excellent resource density. This server is well-suited for database hosting, parallel data processing, static web serving, and moderate-scale application hosting.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-38xCPU-192GB is a KVM virtual server with 38 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 500 GB SSD, delivering top-tier database and single-core performance for enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-density virtual server combining thirty-eight AMD EPYC cores, substantial memory, and local solid-state storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 38 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
192 GB of system memory providing 5.05 GB per core
500 GB of local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier performance in database operations and single-core processing
Optimized for database hosting, web serving, and parallel data compression
